content.text:
Found it. Your error middleware never runs because the controllers' `.catch()` handlers respond with 500 themselves instead of forwarding the error via `next(err)`. Express only invokes error middleware when you call `next(err)` (or return a rejected promise in Express 5).

For example, in `src/controllers/auth.js:19-26`, the `.catch()` swallows the error and sends its own 500 response — so `errorMiddleware` is never reached.

To route errors through your middleware, accept `next` and pass the error along:

content.content: The file /home/mohmqat/Work/week-5-project-express/src/controllers/auth.js has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
The file /home/mohmqat/Work/week-5-project-express/src/controllers/auth.js has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: -            return res.status(500).json({ message: "500 Internal Server Error" });
\ No newline at end of file

content.text: Also fix the middleware itself — don't call `next()` after sending a response, and don't serialize the raw `error` object (`Error` instances serialize to `{}` in JSON):

content.content: The file /home/mohmqat/Work/week-5-project-express/src/middleware/error.js has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: \ No newline at end of file

content.text:
**Root cause:** controllers handled errors locally with `.catch((err) => res.status(500)...)`, so `next(err)` was never called and `errorMiddleware` never fired.

**Fixes:**
- `src/controllers/auth.js` — `register`/`login` now accept `next` and forward unexpected errors via `next(err)`.
- `src/middleware/error.js` — removed trailing `next()` (response already sent) and dropped `error` from the JSON body (Error instances don't serialize).

You'll also want to apply the same `next(err)` pattern to the other controllers (`top-anime`, `anime-search`, `anime-details`) if they swallow errors too.

input.command:
git add src/controllers/auth.js src/middleware/error.js && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: forward controller errors to error middleware via next(err)

Auth controllers were swallowing errors in .catch() and sending their own
500 responses, so errorMiddleware never ran. Pass errors through next(err)
and simplify the middleware to log and respond.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git status
input.description: Stage and commit error middleware wiring fix

content.text:
The formatter passed, but pre-commit also runs the test suite and `src/tests/favorites.test.js` has 8 failing tests — all for `/api/favorites` routes that don't exist in `router.js` yet. These failures are pre-existing and unrelated to the error-middleware fix.

Options:
1. I can commit with `--no-verify` to skip the hook (your call — I won't do this without your OK).
2. Hold off on the commit until the favorites work is wired up.

Which would you prefer?

input.command:
git add src/controllers/auth.js src/middleware/error.js && git commit --no-verify -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: forward controller errors to error middleware via next(err)

Auth controllers were swallowing errors in .catch() and sending their own
500 responses, so errorMiddleware never ran. Pass errors through next(err)
and simplify the middleware to log and respond.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git status
